description:
## Context

`bw` correctly resolves commondir when running inside a git worktree (fixed in bw-xj5, bw-i9g.1). Unit tests in `gitdir_test.go` cover the resolution logic. But there is no acceptance test that actually creates a worktree and runs `bw` commands from it.

This matters because the octopus Elixir ODB had this exact bug — refs written to the per-worktree gitdir were invisible from the main checkout. `bw` is safe today, but a regression would be silent and hard to diagnose.

## What to test

Add a test to `test/acceptance_test.go` (or a new `test/worktree_test.go`) that:

1. Creates a temp git repo with a remote (same pattern as `TestGoldenBasicScenario`)
2. Runs `bw init` from the main checkout
3. Creates a git worktree (`git worktree add ../wt -b wt-branch`)
4. From inside the worktree, runs:
   - `bw -C <worktree-path> create -t task "worktree test ticket"` — creates a ticket
   - `bw -C <worktree-path> list` — lists tickets (verifies read path)
5. From the **main checkout**, verifies:
   - `bw list` shows the ticket created from the worktree
   - The beadwork branch ref in `.git/refs/heads/beadwork` (or packed-refs) reflects the worktree commit
6. Cleans up the worktree

## Key assertion

The ref written by `bw` from within the worktree must be visible from the main checkout. This is the exact failure mode that bit octopus — commits landed in the shared ODB but the ref was invisible.

## Prior art

- `test/acceptance_test.go` — existing golden-path acceptance test pattern
- `internal/repo/gitdir_test.go` — unit tests for worktree gitdir resolution
title: Add acceptance test: bw operations from a git worktree
